The ultimate recipe for soft-baked chocolate chip cookies that are chewy on the inside, crispy on the edges, and loaded with rich chocolate pools.
1 cup unsalted butter, melted and cooled
2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips or chunks
Preheat your oven to 325°F (165°C) and line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat the melted butter, brown sugar, and white sugar together until smooth and well combined.
Beat in the vanilla extract, whole egg, and egg yolk until the mixture is light and creamy.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing just until combined. Do not overmix.
Fold in the chocolate chunks or chips using a wooden spoon or spatula.
Drop large spoonfuls of dough (about 2 tablespoons each) onto the prepared baking sheets, leaving about 2 inches of space between them.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown but the centers are still soft.
Remove from the oven and let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire cooling rack to cool completely.

These chocolate chip cookies are a household favorite. Using melted butter instead of softened butter ensures a much chewier texture, and adding an extra egg yolk binds the dough beautifully while adding rich moisture. Pair them with a cold glass of milk for the perfect afternoon treat!

An authentic, rich, and comforting Italian lasagna made with layers of homemade meat sauce, creamy ricotta, mozzarella, and lasagna sheets.
In a large pot, cook the ground beef, sausage, onion, and garlic over medium heat until browned. Drain excess fat.

Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, tomato sauce, and water. Season with sugar, basil, fennel seeds, Italian seasoning, 1 tbsp salt, and black pepper. Simmer covered for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
In a medium bowl, combine ricotta cheese with the egg and a pinch of salt. Mix well and set aside.
To assemble, spread 1 1/2 cups of the meat sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Arrange 6 lasagna noodles over the meat sauce.
Spread half of the ricotta cheese mixture over the noodles. Top with a third of the mozzarella cheese and 1/4 cup Parmesan.
Spoon another 1 1/2 cups of meat sauce over the cheese. Repeat the layers with remaining noodles, ricotta mixture, another third of mozzarella, meat sauce, and Parmesan.
Top with the remaining meat sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan cheese. Cover with foil (tent it slightly so it doesn't touch the cheese).
Bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil, and bake for an additional 25 minutes until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden. Let cool 15 minutes before slicing and serving.

Lasagna is the ultimate comfort food. This recipe uses a mixture of beef and sweet Italian sausage, simmered slowly, to create a deeply flavorful bolognese sauce. Preparing it a day in advance and baking it the next day makes it taste even better, as the flavors have time to meld together.

A classic Greek salad recipe that is refreshing, simple, and packed with fresh cucumbers, juicy tomatoes, olives, onions, and feta cheese.
3 large ripe tomatoes, cut into wedges
1 English cucumber, sliced into thick half-moons
1 red onion, thinly sliced into rings
1 green bell pepper, chopped into bite-sized pieces
7 oz block of high-quality feta cheese
In a large shallow bowl or platter, arrange the tomato wedges, cucumber slices, green pepper pieces, and red onion rings.
Scatter the Kalamata olives evenly over the top of the vegetables.
Place the block of feta cheese directly on top of the salad (traditional Greek style), or crumble it gently over the salad if preferred.
Whisk the extra virgin olive oil and red wine vinegar in a small bowl, then drizzle it evenly over the entire salad.
Sprinkle the dried oregano and a pinch of sea salt over the feta cheese and vegetables.
Serve immediately at room temperature with crusty bread to soak up the delicious juices.

Horiatiki, or traditional Greek Salad, is eaten all over Greece during summer. Unlike Western versions, authentic Greek salad does not contain lettuce. The key to the best Greek salad is using high-quality ingredients, particularly a good extra virgin olive oil and a block of sheep's milk feta cheese rather than pre-crumbled varieties.
